Definition: An enterprise interaction paradigm in which users express business intent and governed intelligent systems plan, orchestrate, and execute work across enterprise systems.
The layer that interprets intent, resolves context, applies policy, plans work, and coordinates authorized enterprise execution.
The product role fulfilled by HANA-X Copilot between users and enterprise systems of record.
The authoritative enterprise system retaining trusted transactional and master data.
The HANA-X runtime foundation for orchestration, tools, memory, governance, observability, and model routing.
The enterprise intent layer and product experience for natural-language interaction and governed execution with SAP S/4HANA.
An SAP architecture strategy that minimizes modifications to the ERP core and uses released extension and integration mechanisms.
A control model in which designated people review, approve, intervene in, or override consequential agent actions.
A protocol for connecting AI applications to tools and contextual data through standardized interfaces.
An event-based protocol for connecting agent backends and interactive frontends.
